evals.expected_output: Profile summary for the openai handle with bio, follower/following counts, and verified status.
assertions: The run calls GET https://api.scrapecreators.com/v1/twitter/profile with handle=openai
The output reports follower and following counts
Both steps use rail routed via the SELAT Router

evals.prompt: Show me Sam Altman's recent tweets on X.
evals.expected_output: A list of recent tweets for handle sama with engagement metrics.
assertions: The run calls GET https://api.scrapecreators.com/v1/twitter/user-tweets with handle=sama
The output includes tweet text and like/retweet/reply counts
The handle is passed without a leading @

evals.prompt: What has Anthropic been posting on Twitter lately?
evals.expected_output: Recent tweets for handle AnthropicAI plus profile context.
assertions: The run resolves the handle to AnthropicAI
Per-step maxAmount is 0.02 and full-run maxAmount is 0.04

evals.prompt: Pull a trimmed feed of recent tweets for handle nasa.
evals.expected_output: Trimmed tweets response for handle nasa.

evals.prompt: Look up the protected/private X account @someprivateuser.
evals.expected_output: An explanation that protected/private accounts cannot be scraped and the run returns an error with no workaround.
assertions: The output states private/protected accounts cannot be accessed
No claim of successful private-data retrieval is made

evals.prompt: Do a free 402 probe of the twitter profile endpoint without paying.
evals.expected_output: A selat-pay --probe-only command against the Scrape Creators twitter/profile endpoint that returns 402 details without settling payment.
assertions: The command uses selat-pay GET with --probe-only
No payment is settled during the probe
